arts.ttl: One change as Frank Lampard names Coventry City team to face Watford
arts.sum: City: Oliver Dovin; Bobby Thomas, Joel Latibeaudiere, Liam Kitching; Milan van Ewijk, Jamie Allen, Victor Torp, Jake Bidwell; Jack Rudoni; Brandon Thomas-Asante, Ellis Simms. Get the latest Coventry City news sent straight to your phone by joining our Sky Blues WhatsApp group.

arts.ttl: Coventry City v Watford live updates and team news from the CBS Arena
arts.sum: Get the latest Coventry City news sent straight to your phone by joining our Sky Blues WhatsApp group. Get the latest Coventry City headlines sent straight to your phone

arts.ttl: Watford owner forced into humiliating Tom Cleverley U-turn after fan backlash
arts.sum: The future of Watford manager Tom Cleverley was bizarrely - but perhaps not unsurprisingly - thrust into the spotlight this week amid reports that former Villarreal boss Jose Martin Rojo was being lined up

arts.ttl: Coventry City transfer news as Fulham make 'interest known' for player 'not short of admirers'
arts.sum: The 24-year-old full-back has already been the subject of transfer speculation in the current window after Dutch outlet soccernews.nl claimed Turkish club Galatasaray were in talks with the Sky Blues about the player, while the former Netherlands Under-21 international was also linked with interest from Feyenoord and PSV in the summer. Fulham boss Marco Silva is “considering” making a move for Coventry City right-back Milan van Ewijk this month amid uncertain futures for two of his current defenders, it has been claimed.

arts.ttl: Coventry City pair handed 'clean slate' in make or break final days of the transfer window
arts.sum: Asked what’s the plan for the pair, whether it’s a case of assessing them now that they’re back in the fold before deciding whether to re-direct them out on another loan or keep them in the squad for the remainder of the season, Lampard said: “Exactly that.” Asked if the young players have been excited to come back to work with him as the new boss, he said: “I hope so and, yeah, they’re our players and a change of manager makes them wonder what’s happening.

arts.ttl: Frank Lampard makes transfer admission as Coventry City continue to be linked with non-league stars
arts.sum: Asked about the fact that City have been linked with the non-league lads and whether are actively looking to boost the development squad with a view to progressing into the first team, Lampard said: “Yeah, and I don’t know who those names are because I don’t read all the press. Frank Lampard admits Coventry City are looking to boost the club’s development squad with a view to bridging the gap to his first team.

Ellis Simms and Brandon Thomas-Asante scored either side of half time in midweek as Coventry sealed a 2-0 away win against playoff contenders Blackburn. Frank Lampard's men have won back-to-back leagu...

arts.ttl: Milan van Ewijk: Fulham weigh up move to sign Championship defender after ending Emerson Royal interest
arts.sum: The defender is not short of admirers and the Cottagers have made their interest known

arts.ttl: Coventry City injury latest on Wilson, Wright, Mason-Clark and Sheaf
arts.sum: Frank Lampard has provided an injury update on Coventry City’s leading scorer Haji Wright whose return to action is taking a “bit longer” than had been hoped. I haven’t seen him yet and just see him in the gym and have a little chat with him here and there, so it’s really unfortunate because I know the level of player that he is and what he can do for this club.

arts.ttl: Frank Lampard provides transfer update as Coventry City's Rabbi Matondo deal with Rangers uncertain
arts.sum: Get the latest Coventry City news sent straight to your phone by joining our Sky Blues WhatsApp group. Frank Lampard says there’s nothing “imminent” in terms of in-coming Coventry City transfers amid reports that the deal for Rangers’ forward Rabbi Matondo has broken down.
